title: Thread an actor argument through a 4-layer call chain into the audit log
task: This task-tracker writes an audit record for every mutation, but the records do not say WHO did it. Add an `actor` (a string) to every audit entry. The public operations in src/operations.js — createTask(title), completeTask(id), reopenTask(id), deleteTask(id) — must each take an additional final `actor` argument, and that actor must be threaded down through the call chain (operations -> service.js -> store.js -> audit.js) so that every recorded entry includes the correct { action, taskId, actor }. Do not change the audit record's other fields or the existing task behavior, and do not modify anything under test/.
notes: Discriminator: long-horizon plumbing across a deep multi-file call chain (4 layers, 4 operations). Missing the actor at any hop leaves entries with actor undefined -> deepStrictEqual fails. file_contains pins force the thread through each layer; the test pin blocks tampering.
